tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto is where you land first — find the glasses shops before the jet lag kicks in.
tasks.reward: Locks in early-trip shopping so you can wear new glasses for the rest of Japan.
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan. I'm arriving March 23, 2026 and want to buy prescription glasses early in the trip so I can wear them. I need 4 pairs total during the trip. Look for: stores with English-speaking staff, same-day service, good selection of frames, and reasonable prices. Provide store names, neighborhoods, Google Maps links, and opening hours. I prefer areas near central Kyoto hotels.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance agent with subject 'Japan glasses coverage question'
tasks.hook_line: 7 days out — need to know if insurance covers international purchases before you're standing in a Tokyo shop.
tasks.reward: Removes the 'can I actually do this?' question before the trip starts.
nuggets: Some US insurance covers international opticals
Prescription valid 1-2 years in most countries
Receipt + prescription usually enough for reimbursement
tasks.system_prompt: Help me draft an email to my insurance provider asking about coverage for eyeglasses purchased in Japan. I'm traveling March 23 - April 7, 2026 and planning to buy 4 pairs of prescription glasses. I need to know: (1) Does my vision insurance cover international purchases? (2) What documentation do I need (receipts, prescription, itemized invoices)? (3) Is there a reimbursement process? (4) Any limits on number of pairs? Keep the email concise and professional.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'top temples Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left.
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto, Japan (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy spots. We're okay with early mornings to beat crowds.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'things to do Takayama Japan spring'
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the mountain town wildcard — what even is there beyond old streets?
tasks.reward: Completes the middle section of the trip — just Tokyo research left.
nuggets: Old town walking district free entry
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2 days in Takayama, Japan (March 28-29, 2026). It's a mountain town in the Japanese Alps. Traveling with Dana. We like hiking, nature, local food, and exploring less touristy areas. Already have hotel booked. I want a day-by-day plan with: morning markets, hiking options (beginner-friendly, 2-4 hours), old town highlights, and good local restaurants. Include opening hours, Google Maps links, and any seasonal considerations for late March.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'best neighborhoods Tokyo'
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is massive — 8 days to explore without a plan is chaos waiting to happen.
tasks.reward: Finishes all Japan trip planning — ready to go wheels-up.
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 8 days in Tokyo, Japan (March 30 - April 6, 2026). Traveling with Dana. We like: quirky neighborhoods, good food (ramen, sushi, street food), museums, shopping (stationery, fountain pens, watches, eyeglasses), and a mix of modern/traditional. Already planning to visit limited edition fountain pen shops. I want a loose day-by-day framework (not rigid schedule) covering different neighborhoods, with highlights, opening hours, Google Maps links, and transit tips. Include both tourist spots and local favorites.

tasks.first_domino: Open terminal and clone the Cerebro repo
tasks.hook_line: Developer portals are never easy to spin up — can you crack this one?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all future Cerebro work — portal becomes explorable.
nuggets: Check README for Docker setup steps
Environment variables needed for local auth
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running locally. It's a work project for internal engineering tools. I need to: (1) Clone the repo, (2) Install dependencies, (3) Set up any required environment variables or config, (4) Run the development server. Walk me through likely setup steps for a typical Node.js/React developer portal, including common gotchas like Docker containers, database setup, authentication mocks, and port conflicts. Assume I'm comfortable with terminal but haven't touched this codebase before.

recommended_slots.reason: 7 days out, low barrier research task perfect for morning ramp-up energy before work hours.
Quick errand that unblocks glasses shopping confidence — fits medium effort transition slot.
Curiosity-driven research with clear outcome — good for sustained focus when energy peaks later.
